sections.title: Creating Wikidata Items
sections.description: Step-by-step guides to add new entities to Wikidata's knowledge graph — from label and description to linked statements and references.

articles.title: How to add 'augmented journalism' to Wikidata

item.label: augmented journalism
item.description: journalistic practice combining human expertise with artificial intelligence to assist reporting, data analysis and fact-checking while keeping editorial judgment human

steps.name: Create the item
steps.instructions: Fill in Language (en), Label, Description and Aliases (pipe-separated), then click 'Create'. Wikidata assigns the item a unique QID.

fields.label: augmented journalism
fields.description: journalistic practice combining human expertise with artificial intelligence to assist reporting, data analysis and fact-checking while keeping editorial judgment human

steps.name: Add statement: subclass of → journalism
steps.instructions: In the Statements section, click '+ add statement'. Select the property, select the value, then click 'publish'.

steps.name: Add statement: uses → artificial intelligence
steps.instructions: Click '+ add statement' again. Select the property, select the value, then click 'publish'.

steps.name: Add a reference (recommended)
steps.instructions: Under each statement, click 'add reference'. Add a reference URL and the retrieval date, then click 'publish'. References protect new items from deletion for lack of notability.

reference_properties.label: reference URL
reference_properties.value: URL of a source supporting the claim

articles.result: The item is searchable on Wikidata, linked to the knowledge graph as a subclass of journalism (Q11030) that uses artificial intelligence (Q11660), and usable for entity linking via its QID.
